English Wed, 20.05.2026 - Thu, 21.05.2026 various locations, Vienna, - - Add to calendar alert('Url wurde in die Zwischnablage kopiert.'))"> Share event p]:mb-0"> How are digital technologies shaping the future of cultural experiences? On 20 and 21 May 2026, Creative Days Vienna will once again bring together leading international creative professionals for a diverse program of talks, tours, performances, installations and networking formats. The participants of Creative Days Vienna 2026 will provide substantial impulses for architecture, design, film, gaming, fashion, music and the visual arts. Together, we will discuss the interplay between technological innovations, social change and creative industries. Creative Days Vienna 2026 will open with an (Un)Conference exploring how creatives can reshape cultural and digital infrastructures. Provocations by Nadia Piet (AIxDesign), Calum Bowden (Trust) and Christie Morgan (SOFTER) set the tone, followed by workshops and discussions. The opening features keynotes by Shumon Basar and Günseli Yalcinkaya, as well as performances, installations and a club night. Day 2 brings workshops led by Paloma Oliveira, Ramon Reichert, NeverAtHome, Subotron, Marlène Delrive, Area 404 and a Tour presenting Biofabrique Vienna and Creatives for Vienna projects. Get your Tickets here . PROGRAM DAY 1 (UN)CONFERENCE 1.30 - 6.30 pm (Un)Conference “Creating the culture we want / Building the infrastructures we need” The (Un)Conference features lightning talks from Nadia Piet on alternative AI practices, Calum Bowden on collaborative system change, and Christie Morgan on softer digital futures. Followed by Workshops that invite collective exploration, concluding with a final reflection. Find more information and how to apply here . (limited capacity) 5.30 - 6.00 pm Networking / Food / Experiences @ Funkhaus Vienna Participants are invited to network and connect over food and drinks. This informal space is meant for exchanging insights from the (Un)Conference and beyond, meeting fellow creatives, and experiencing installations before the evening program begins. CONTENT VIENNA SCREENINGS 6.00 - 6.30 pm Content Vienna Talk & Screenings: Merve Sahin and Total View Two Content Vienna-awarded projects present how digital tools uncover hidden layers of cities, architecture and society. “Merging Visions” and “Sensing Sinicization” are introduced through short talks and videos, offering insights into artistic and investigative digital practices. Find more information about the event here . OPENING 6.30 - 11.00 pm OPENING Creative Days Vienna 2026 @ Funkhaus Vienna The opening begins with a keynote by Shumon Basar and a talk from Günseli Yalcinkaya. An interactive installation curated by Node Media Lab accompanies the evening, alongside a performance of Cat Jimenez curated by Never At Home. The program concludes with a club night at Funkhaus Vienna. Find more information about the event here . DAY 2 MORNING SESSIONS 10.00 am – 12.30 pm WORKSHOP: Open Source: Building equitable, inclusive and sustainable tech communities @ Westbahnstudios This workshop by Paloma Oliveira and Kristina Maurer explores open source as a cultural and political force, offering tools for building equitable, decentralised and collaborative digital systems. Participants will trace its history, study inspiring projects and shape their own visions for inclusive, sustainable digital futures. It is aimed at creatives, developers and technologists. Find more information and how to apply here . 9.30 am - 1.30 pm WORKSHOP: Shared Infrastructures. tools & governance for creative spaces with Ramón Reichert and NeverAtHome The workshop gathers creatives to identify practical needs and develop empowering spaces for action. After input on ownership, platform economies and data use, participants co-create alternative approaches for non commercial digital environments and sustainable practices. Hosted by NeverAtHome. Find more information and how to apply here . AFTERNOON SESSIONS 2.00 - 4.30 pm WORKSHOP: Mapping Biofabrique Vienna. Between Habitat and Demolition Led by architect Bika Rebek (Some Place Studio), this workshop explores Biofabrique Vienna’s showcase on circular materials through mapping, sensory observation and 3D scanning, creating a shared digital archive of temporary materials and setups.Find more information and how to apply here . 3.00 - 6.00 pm Talk, Panel & Networking: Digital Design for Health @ MuseumsQuartier Game designer Marlène Delrive presents As Long As You’re Here, a game that conveys the experience of Alzheimer’s from the inside. A panel with Jutta Scheibelberger, Georg Russegger and moderator Jogi Neufeld explores how games, wearables and digital tools can support patient‑centred health solutions. The event concludes with exchange and networking. In cooperation with SUBOTRON. Find more information and how to register here . 5.00 - 7.00 pm WORKSHOP: Fair P[l]ay by AREA[404] @ Westbahnstudios A hands-on workshop exploring the political economy of music distribution and the alternatives emerging beyond dominant streaming platforms. Participants trace music’s material history, examine cooperative and open-source models, and work in groups to design new distribution approaches. The workshop is for musicians and creatives seeking fairer ways to shape ownership, audiences and value. Find more information and how to apply here . TOURS 5.00 - 6.30 pm TOUR: Creatives for Vienna X Biofabrique Vienna @ Nordwestbahnhof This tour visits the Nordwestbahnhof, an area undergoing major urban transformation. The Biofabrique Vienna presents its new site and its work on bioregional material innovation. Two winning projects from the “Creatives for Vienna – Making Spaces” competition showcase how local creative initiatives enrich public spaces and support the city in responding to social and climate challenges. https://viennaup.flave.world/user/events.text: Programme Log in Creative Catalyst Aperture To book a ticket please kindly register or log in here . Tickets MORNING: Welcome + Talks Join us for our morning session (9:00- 13:00) full of inspiring insights from industry experts and creatives working across the creative sector. FREE TICKET Additional Tickets Only available with the purchase of at least one mandatory ticket. AFTERNOON: Mentoring Sessions & Eu Funding Updates for Partners In the afternoon, we offer both a program partner workshop as well as mentoring sessions developed by Morningsloth: For program partners, we offer a focus session (Track A) around European Funding updates and mutual exchange, led by EU funding experts Jan Vincent Jordan and others. For Program prospects, we offer smaller sessions with mentors (Track B) of the Creative Catalyst network: coaches, investors, and industry experts working directly with them on their next steps and answering burning questions that arise. Through these sessions, participants can also learn more about current opportunities from EIT Culture & Creativity and explore how to engage with upcoming programs and funding calls that fit their trajectories. When selecting this ticket, please indicate which track you will be part of. FREE TICKET Description What does it take to bring a creative business to European scale? Aperture is the launch of Creative Catalyst Austria, an innovation and growth pathway programme for Austrian creative businesses extending themselves beyond borders. The program is specifically designed to grow existing creative businesses beyond local markets, into European scale. This event gives you a preview of the Creative Catalyst and the journeys it offers: from fruitful provocations, industry insights and foresight, all the way to collaboration building, networking and individual mentoring. The morning is dedicated to talks from industry experts working at the intersection of materials and creative technology, such as industry and innovation expert Elsa Parente of Positive Materials , autonomous system expert Joanna Bakas of Frontira , Brand Developer Claudia Chmielowiec of BioFluff , as well as designer and material scientist Charles Gateau of Studio LoopLoop . They will share their perspectives and experiences on emerging shifts within Europe's creative economy, and how creative businesses can position themselves within them. In the afternoon, the focus turns to engagement and connection with a programme partner workshop and mentoring sessions developed by Morningsloth : For programme partners, we offer a focus session around European Funding updates and mutual exchange, led by EU funding experts Jan Vincent Jordan , and others. For programme prospects, we offer smaller sessions with mentors of the Creative Catalyst network: coaches, investors, and industry experts working directly with them on their next steps and answering burning questions that arise. Through these sessions, participants can also learn more about current opportunities from EIT Culture & Creativity and explore how to engage with upcoming programmes and funding calls that fit their trajectories. We kindly invite all participants to join us to a session of Boule and drinks after the event.
